title: lonchocarpus kerberi
intro: Lonchocarpus kerberi, also known as the African moon tree, is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a toxic compound called rotenone, a natural insecticide, and a non-toxic compound called kerberine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus kerberi include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Traditional herbal preparations of Lonchocarpus kerberi include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat a range of conditions.
